Delayed entry for the storms Sunday night June 16 through Monday morning June 17. Social media report with pictures of damage to a cattle feeder and a dented grain bin. Report also mentioned damaged/twisted trees. They were unsure of the time. Radar imagery does indicate a MARC signature which would imply strong wind gusts at the surface. Time estimated from radar of 2:45 to 2:55 AM on June 17.

Delayed report from Monday morning storms. Social media report with video of multiple trees damaged. Branch size varied, but there were several branches estimated 6-8 inches in diameter, and a few 12+ inch limbs down. Report estimated damage occurred between 2:30-3:10 am. Radar confirms time estimate to be around 2:50 when the strongest winds would have been at that location.
Measured by personal weather station.

Numerous trees and branches down in the city of Lake Park. Two cars are damaged at one residence and at least two homes have branches or trees on them. Time estimated based on radar and personal weather station report of 56 mph for several minutes.
4 foot diameter cottonwood tree uprooted. Time estimated based on radar.
Measured by a personal weather station.

Water over 443rd 2 miles north of Hwy 18. Numerous gravel rural roads also underwater.

Numerous streets under water after report of nearly 4 inches of rain through 7 pm.
Multiple roads have been reported closed due to standing water. The Healy Dam has also ruptured taking out a dock downstream towards the American Creek Marina.

Corrects previous flash flood report source. Per social media, no travel advised in Mitchell due to flooding. Many reports of basements flooded in town. Portions of I-90 near Mitchell closed. Numerous stranded motorists.
Reported via social media. Some roads flooded and barricaded. Some homes having flooded basements and/or sewer backups.

Delayed reports... Several flooded streets across the city. Pictures show depth anywhere from curb high to as deep as tire deep in some locations, including Nicollet St, E 23rd/C St, Garretson/Hennepin and Jackson St. Pulaski Park was also flooded. Automated rain gauge reports suggest anywhere from 2-4 inches fell across the city, with localized higher amounts.
